references.hebrew: וְזֹאת֩ לְפָנִ֨ים בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל עַל־הַגְּאֻלָּ֤ה וְעַל־הַתְּמוּרָה֙ לְקַיֵּ֣ם כׇּל־דָּבָ֔ר שָׁלַ֥ף אִ֛ישׁ נַעֲל֖וֹ וְנָתַ֣ן לְרֵעֵ֑הוּ וְזֹ֥את הַתְּעוּדָ֖ה בְּיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
references.english: Now this was formerly done in Israel in cases of redemption or exchange: to validate any transaction, one party would take off a sandal and hand it to the other. Such was the practiceepractice Understanding Heb. teʻudah in the sense of the Arabic ʻādah and Syriac ʻyādā. Cf. Ibn Ezra. in Israel.

references.hebrew: וַיִּ֩בֶן֩ יְהֹוָ֨ה אֱלֹהִ֧ים <small>׀</small> אֶֽת־הַצֵּלָ֛ע אֲשֶׁר־לָקַ֥ח מִן־הָֽאָדָ֖ם לְאִשָּׁ֑ה וַיְבִאֶ֖הָ אֶל־הָֽאָדָֽם׃
references.english: And the E<small>TERNAL</small> God fashioned the side that had been taken from the Human into a woman, bringing her to the Human.
